steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between sentences
steps.content: The sentence before the blank calls the 480 BCE eclipse “annular” without defining the term. The blank sentence then explains what annular means: the Moon didn’t cover the Sun completely, creating a ring of fire instead. The relationship is definition: the second sentence restates and clarifies a specific word just used in the first sentence.

steps.title: Match the relationship to the right transition
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘That is,’: signals a restatement or definition of something just stated — exactly the role played here (defining “annular”). ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Nonetheless,’: signals continuation despite an obstacle or concession; no obstacle is present, so this creates a false contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Meanwhile,’: signals events happening simultaneously; the blank sentence defines a term, it does not describe a parallel event. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘For example,’: signals a specific instance of a general claim; the blank sentence defines “annular,” it does not give one example among many. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
